payload.design_note: Navigation is HYBRID and graphic_intensity is graphic_heavy. Why Machines Learn is expository, AI-topical, math-dense: the 14 sections read best in author sequence (each algorithm builds on the prior chapter's linear-algebra and calculus scaffolding), so the primary chrome is a linear sticky sidebar TOC with IntersectionObserver scroll-spy and a 3px scroll progress bar. But the concepts are also reference material a technically literate reader hunts by topic, so the TOC doubles as a thematic jump-table grouped (Foundations, Classical ML, Networks, Deep Learning). Body type is a humanist serif at 66ch measure, line-height 1.65, fluid clamp size, with a dark-mode pair (#e6e6e6 on #1a1a1a) via prefers-color-scheme. Because this is the visual-first category, every concept chapter externalizes its mathematics: 20 PNG equation scans are reconstructed as self-contained inline SVG in clean mathematical notation (no external images), and 11 architecture/process scans become inline-SVG diagrams (visual-cortex pipelines, convolution and pooling grids, LeNet/CNN stacks, bias-variance and double-descent curves). The required expository trio is satisfied: a model-comparison table (classical-ML approaches), a field timeline (perceptron-to-LLM milestones), and many system/sequence diagrams. Equations sit in inset tinted blocks with a math-serif/monospace fallback for clarity. Channel B is disabled (A_only) per intake; no decorative imagery ships.

illustration_plan.description: Perceptron decision hyperplane equation w·x + b = 0
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="w dot x plus b equals 0"><text x="200" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="30" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">∑<tspan font-size="18" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> w<tspan font-size="18" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan>x<tspan font-size="18" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> + b = 0</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Perceptron convergence bound M ≤ R²/γ²
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 90" width="400" height="90" role="img" aria-label="M is less than or equal to R squared over gamma squared"><text x="150" y="55" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="32" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">M ≤</text><line x1="170" y1="48" x2="250" y2="48" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="210" y="38" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">R²</text><text x="210" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">γ²</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Mean-squared-error cost function J = E(e_n squared)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="J equals expectation of e n squared"><text x="200" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="30" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">J = E[ e<tspan font-size="18" baseline-shift="sub">n</tspan><tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="18">2</tspan> ]</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Bayes theorem P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 100" width="400" height="100" role="img" aria-label="P of A given B equals P of B given A times P of A over P of B"><text x="130" y="58" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">P(A|B) =</text><line x1="145" y1="50" x2="360" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="252" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">P(B|A) · P(A)</text><text x="252" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">P(B)</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Bernoulli PMF P(x=k) = p^k (1-p)^(1-k)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="P of x equals k equals p to the k times one minus p to the one minus k"><text x="200" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">P(x=k) = p<tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="18">k</tspan> (1−p)<tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="18">1−k</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Volume of unit ball in d dimensions using the Gamma function
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 110" width="400" height="110" role="img" aria-label="Volume of d-ball equals pi to the d over 2 over Gamma of d over 2 plus 1"><text x="120" y="62" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">V<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">d</tspan> =</text><line x1="135" y1="55" x2="360" y2="55" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="247" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">π<tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="15">d/2</tspan></text><text x="247" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">Γ( d/2 + 1 )</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: SVM margin constraint y_i (w·x_i + b) ≥ 1
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="y i times open paren w dot x i plus b close paren is greater than or equal to 1"><text x="200" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">y<tspan font-size="18" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> ( w·x<tspan font-size="18" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> + b ) ≥ 1</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Optimal weight vector w = sum alpha_i y_i x_i
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 75" width="400" height="75" role="img" aria-label="w equals sum over i of alpha i y i x i"><text x="200" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">w = ∑<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> α<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> y<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> x<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: RBF kernel K(a,b) = exp(-||a-b||^2 / 2 sigma^2)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 95" width="400" height="95" role="img" aria-label="K of a b equals exp of negative norm of a minus b squared over 2 sigma squared"><text x="110" y="55" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">K(a,b) = exp</text><text x="118" y="55" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="40" fill="currentColor">(</text><text x="135" y="55" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="30" fill="currentColor">−</text><line x1="160" y1="48" x2="335" y2="48" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="247" y="38" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="22" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">‖a − b‖²</text><text x="247" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="22" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">2σ²</text><text x="345" y="55" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="40" fill="currentColor">)</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Ising Hamiltonian H = -sum_ij J sigma_i sigma_j - sum_j h sigma_j
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 75" width="400" height="75" role="img" aria-label="H equals negative sum over i j of J sigma i sigma j minus sum over j of h sigma j"><text x="200" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">H = −∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i,j</tspan> Jσ<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan>σ<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan> − ∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan> hσ<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Hopfield neuron output y_i = sign(sum_j w_ij y_j)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="y i equals sign of sum over j of w i j y j"><text x="200" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">y<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> = sign( ∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan> w<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">ij</tspan> y<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan> )</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Hopfield energy E = -1/2 sum_i sum_j w_ij y_i y_j
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 75" width="400" height="75" role="img" aria-label="E equals negative one half sum over i sum over j of w i j y i y j"><text x="200" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="25" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">E = −½ ∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan>∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan> w<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">ij</tspan> y<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> y<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">j</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Hebbian weight matrix W = y^T y - I
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="W equals y transpose y minus identity"><text x="200" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">W = y<tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="18">T</tspan> y − I</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Multi-memory storage W = (1/n) sum_i y_i^T y_i - I
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 95" width="400" height="95" role="img" aria-label="W equals one over n sum over i of y i transpose y i minus identity"><text x="105" y="55" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">W =</text><text x="122" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="22" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">1</text><line x1="112" y1="52" x2="132" y2="52" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="122" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="22" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">n</text><text x="270" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> y<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan><tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="16">T</tspan> y<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> − I</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Energy change on neuron flip ΔE = E_new - E_old
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 70" width="400" height="70" role="img" aria-label="delta E equals E new minus E old"><text x="200" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="28" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">ΔE = E<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">new</tspan> − E<tspan font-size="16" baseline-shift="sub">old</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Sigmoid function sigma(z) = 1 / (1 + e^-z)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 95" width="400" height="95" role="img" aria-label="sigma of z equals 1 over 1 plus e to the minus z"><text x="130" y="55" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">σ(z) =</text><text x="250" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">1</text><line x1="160" y1="50" x2="345" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="252" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">1 + e<tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="16">−z</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Network output sum alpha_i sigma(w_i x + b_i)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 75" width="400" height="75" role="img" aria-label="f of x equals sum over i of alpha i sigma of w i dot x plus b i"><text x="200" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="25" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">f(x) = ∑<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> α<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> σ( w<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan>·x + b<tspan font-size="15" baseline-shift="sub">i</tspan> )</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Chain rule dy/dx = (dy/dz)(dz/dx)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 95" width="400" height="95" role="img" aria-label="d y over d x equals d y over d z times d z over d x"><text x="55" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dy</text><line x1="30" y1="50" x2="80" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="55" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dx</text><text x="110" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" fill="currentColor">=</text><text x="165" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dy</text><line x1="140" y1="50" x2="190" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="165" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dz</text><text x="215" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" fill="currentColor">·</text><text x="265" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dz</text><line x1="240" y1="50" x2="290" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="265" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dx</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Sigmoid activation a(z) = 1 / (1 + e^-z) (differentiability context)
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 95" width="400" height="95" role="img" aria-label="a of z equals 1 over 1 plus e to the minus z"><text x="130" y="55" text-anchor="end" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="26" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">a(z) =</text><text x="250" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">1</text><line x1="160" y1="50" x2="345" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="252" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">1 + e<tspan baseline-shift="super" font-size="16">−z</tspan></text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Sigmoid derivative d-sigma/dz = sigma(z)(1 - sigma(z))
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="eq" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 95" width="400" height="95" role="img" aria-label="d sigma over d z equals sigma of z times one minus sigma of z"><text x="50" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dσ</text><line x1="25" y1="50" x2="75" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="24" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">dz</text><text x="250" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="25" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor">= σ(z)·(1 − σ(z))</text></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Visual cortex pathway: retinal ganglion cells feed a simple cell that performs edge detection
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 460 150" width="460" height="150" role="img" aria-label="Retinal ganglion cells connect to a simple cell that detects an edge"><defs><marker id="a4" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="currentColor"/></marker></defs><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="13" fill="currentColor"><circle cx="55" cy="35" r="14" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="55" cy="75" r="14" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="55" cy="115" r="14" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="55" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">RGCs</text><line x1="70" y1="40" x2="195" y2="72" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a4)"/><line x1="70" y1="75" x2="195" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a4)"/><line x1="70" y1="110" x2="195" y2="78" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a4)"/><circle cx="225" cy="75" r="22" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2"/><text x="225" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">simple cell</text><line x1="249" y1="75" x2="330" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a4)"/><rect x="335" y="45" width="60" height="60" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="345" y1="95" x2="385" y2="55" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="3"/><text x="365" y="125" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">edge detected</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Visual cortex pathway: several simple cells feed a complex cell giving translation invariance
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 460 170" width="460" height="170" role="img" aria-label="Several simple cells feed a complex cell that responds regardless of edge position"><defs><marker id="a5" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="currentColor"/></marker></defs><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="currentColor"><circle cx="50" cy="35" r="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="50" cy="85" r="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="50" cy="135" r="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="50" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">simple cells</text><line x1="68" y1="40" x2="245" y2="80" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a5)"/><line x1="68" y1="85" x2="245" y2="85" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a5)"/><line x1="68" y1="130" x2="245" y2="90" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a5)"/><circle cx="275" cy="85" r="26" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2"/><text x="275" y="89" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">complex</text><text x="275" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">cell</text><line x1="303" y1="85" x2="360" y2="85" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a5)"/><text x="410" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">fires for edge</text><text x="410" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">at any position</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: 5x5 image grid with a 2x2 kernel overlaid in the top-left corner
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 200" width="320" height="200" role="img" aria-label="A 5 by 5 pixel grid with a 2 by 2 kernel highlighted over the top-left pixels"><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor"><g stroke="currentColor" fill="none">M0 0<rect x="30" y="20" width="150" height="150"/><line x1="60" y1="20" x2="60" y2="170"/><line x1="90" y1="20" x2="90" y2="170"/><line x1="120" y1="20" x2="120" y2="170"/><line x1="150" y1="20" x2="150" y2="170"/><line x1="30" y1="50" x2="180" y2="50"/><line x1="30" y1="80" x2="180" y2="80"/><line x1="30" y1="110" x2="180" y2="110"/><line x1="30" y1="140" x2="180" y2="140"/></g><rect x="30" y="20" width="60" height="60" fill="rgba(181,67,0,0.18)" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2"/><text x="105" y="15" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">5 × 5 image</text><text x="230" y="45" font-size="12" fill="#b54300">2 × 2 kernel</text><line x1="205" y1="45" x2="92" y2="50" stroke="#b54300"/></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Kernel sliding across the image: three successive positions of the 2x2 window
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 180" width="360" height="180" role="img" aria-label="A 2 by 2 kernel shown at three successive sliding positions across an image grid"><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor"><g stroke="currentColor" fill="none"><rect x="30" y="30" width="180" height="108"/><line x1="66" y1="30" x2="66" y2="138"/><line x1="102" y1="30" x2="102" y2="138"/><line x1="138" y1="30" x2="138" y2="138"/><line x1="174" y1="30" x2="174" y2="138"/><line x1="30" y1="66" x2="210" y2="66"/><line x1="30" y1="102" x2="210" y2="102"/></g><rect x="30" y="30" width="72" height="72" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><rect x="66" y="30" width="72" height="72" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><rect x="102" y="30" width="72" height="72" fill="rgba(181,67,0,0.18)" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="250" y="60" font-size="12">kernel slides</text><text x="250" y="80" font-size="12">left → right,</text><text x="250" y="100" font-size="12">top → bottom</text><text x="120" y="165" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">stride 1 convolution</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Prewitt horizontal and vertical edge-detection 3x3 kernels
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 420 180" width="420" height="180" role="img" aria-label="Two Prewitt 3 by 3 kernels, one for vertical edges and one for horizontal edges"><g font-family="Cambria Math, Georgia, serif" font-size="18" fill="currentColor"><text x="95" y="22" text-anchor="middle" font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12">Vertical (Gₓ)</text><path d="M30 35 L30 145 M40 35 L30 35 M40 145 L30 145" stroke="currentColor" fill="none"/><path d="M160 35 L160 145 M150 35 L160 35 M150 145 L160 145" stroke="currentColor" fill="none"/><text x="55" y="60">-1</text><text x="88" y="60">0</text><text x="118" y="60">+1</text><text x="55" y="98">-1</text><text x="88" y="98">0</text><text x="118" y="98">+1</text><text x="55" y="136">-1</text><text x="88" y="136">0</text><text x="118" y="136">+1</text><text x="325" y="22" text-anchor="middle" font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12">Horizontal (Gᵧ)</text><path d="M250 35 L250 145 M260 35 L250 35 M260 145 L250 145" stroke="currentColor" fill="none"/><path d="M390 35 L390 145 M380 35 L390 35 M380 145 L390 145" stroke="currentColor" fill="none"/><text x="273" y="60">-1</text><text x="306" y="60">-1</text><text x="336" y="60">-1</text><text x="278" y="98">0</text><text x="311" y="98">0</text><text x="344" y="98">0</text><text x="275" y="136">+1</text><text x="308" y="136">+1</text><text x="338" y="136">+1</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: A neuron whose weights are exactly the kernel elements: convolution expressed as a weighted sum
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 180" width="440" height="180" role="img" aria-label="Pixel patch values multiplied by kernel weights feed into a neuron that outputs the convolution sum"><defs><marker id="a18" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="currentColor"/></marker></defs><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="currentColor"><text x="55" y="20" text-anchor="middle">pixels</text><circle cx="40" cy="45" r="10" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="40" cy="85" r="10" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><circle cx="40" cy="125" r="10" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="50" y1="45" x2="250" y2="82" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a18)"/><line x1="50" y1="85" x2="250" y2="85" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a18)"/><line x1="50" y1="125" x2="250" y2="88" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a18)"/><text x="150" y="55" font-size="11" fill="#b54300">w₁ (kernel)</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="11" fill="#b54300">w₂</text><text x="150" y="118" font-size="11" fill="#b54300">w₃</text><circle cx="275" cy="85" r="24" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2"/><text x="275" y="90" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16">Σ</text><line x1="299" y1="85" x2="360" y2="85" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a18)"/><text x="400" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">feature</text><text x="400" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">value</text><text x="220" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">neuron weights = kernel</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: CNN layer hierarchy: Conv to MaxPool to Conv to fully connected output
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 540 130" width="540" height="130" role="img" aria-label="CNN pipeline: convolution, max pooling, convolution, fully connected layer, output"><defs><marker id="a19" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="currentColor"/></marker></defs><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="currentColor" text-anchor="middle"><rect x="10" y="45" width="70" height="45" fill="none" stroke="#b54300"/><text x="45" y="72">Conv</text><line x1="82" y1="67" x2="108" y2="67" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a19)"/><rect x="110" y="45" width="80" height="45" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="150" y="68">MaxPool</text><line x1="192" y1="67" x2="218" y2="67" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a19)"/><rect x="220" y="45" width="70" height="45" fill="none" stroke="#b54300"/><text x="255" y="72">Conv</text><line x1="292" y1="67" x2="318" y2="67" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a19)"/><rect x="320" y="45" width="110" height="45" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="375" y="72">Fully Connected</text><line x1="432" y1="67" x2="458" y2="67" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a19)"/><rect x="460" y="45" width="70" height="45" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="495" y="72">Output</text><text x="270" y="25" font-size="11">edges → textures → parts → objects</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Max pooling: a 2x2 window with stride 2 reduces a 4x4 map to 2x2 by taking the maximum
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 180" width="400" height="180" role="img" aria-label="Max pooling a 4 by 4 grid with a 2 by 2 window and stride 2 produces a 2 by 2 grid of maxima"><defs><marker id="a21" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="currentColor"/></marker></defs><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="13" fill="currentColor" text-anchor="middle"><g stroke="currentColor" fill="none"><rect x="20" y="30" width="120" height="120"/><line x1="50" y1="30" x2="50" y2="150"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="150"/><line x1="110" y1="30" x2="110" y2="150"/><line x1="20" y1="60" x2="140" y2="60"/><line x1="20" y1="90" x2="140" y2="90"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120"/></g><rect x="20" y="30" width="60" height="60" fill="rgba(181,67,0,0.15)" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2"/><text x="35" y="50">1</text><text x="65" y="50">3</text><text x="35" y="80">2</text><text x="65" y="80">9</text><line x1="148" y1="90" x2="205" y2="90" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a21)"/><text x="176" y="80" font-size="11">max</text><g stroke="currentColor" fill="none"><rect x="215" y="60" width="80" height="60"/><line x1="255" y1="60" x2="255" y2="120"/><line x1="215" y1="90" x2="295" y2="90"/></g><text x="235" y="95" fill="#b54300">9</text><text x="180" y="168" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">2×2 window, stride 2</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: LeNet architecture: input image, 5 conv kernels, max pooling, FC layer of 50, FC layer of 10 outputs
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 560 140" width="560" height="140" role="img" aria-label="LeNet: input image, five convolution kernels, max pooling, fully connected layer of fifty, output layer of ten"><defs><marker id="a22" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="7" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L7,3 L0,6 Z" fill="currentColor"/></marker></defs><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" text-anchor="middle"><rect x="10" y="50" width="50" height="50" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="35" y="118">input</text><line x1="62" y1="75" x2="88" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a22)"/><rect x="90" y="45" width="60" height="60" fill="none" stroke="#b54300"/><text x="120" y="78">5 kernels</text><text x="120" y="122">Conv</text><line x1="152" y1="75" x2="178" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a22)"/><rect x="180" y="52" width="60" height="46" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="210" y="79">MaxPool</text><line x1="242" y1="75" x2="268" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a22)"/><rect x="270" y="40" width="70" height="70" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="305" y="79">FC · 50</text><line x1="342" y1="75" x2="368" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a22)"/><rect x="370" y="52" width="60" height="46" fill="none" stroke="#b54300"/><text x="400" y="79">FC · 10</text><line x1="432" y1="75" x2="458" y2="75" stroke="currentColor" marker-end="url(#a22)"/><text x="510" y="72">digit</text><text x="510" y="88">0–9</text><text x="280" y="20" font-size="12" font-weight="bold">LeNet</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Classic bias-variance tradeoff U-curve of test error against model complexity
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 420 240" width="420" height="240" role="img" aria-label="Bias-variance tradeoff: test error forms a U-shaped curve as model complexity increases, with bias falling and variance rising"><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="currentColor"><line x1="50" y1="200" x2="400" y2="200" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="50" y1="200" x2="50" y2="20" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="225" y="230" text-anchor="middle">model complexity →</text><text x="15" y="110" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90 15 110)">error →</text><path d="M65 50 C140 150 180 185 225 185 C290 185 340 120 390 45" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2.5"/><path d="M65 60 C160 140 260 185 390 195" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5 4"/><path d="M65 195 C200 188 300 130 390 45" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 3"/><circle cx="225" cy="185" r="4" fill="#b54300"/><text x="225" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#b54300">sweet spot</text><text x="330" y="70" font-size="11">test error</text><text x="330" y="185" font-size="10">bias² ↓</text><text x="330" y="110" font-size="10">variance ↑</text></g></svg>

illustration_plan.description: Double descent curve: test error rises to the interpolation threshold then descends again in the overparameterized regime
illustration_plan.svg_inline: <svg class="diagram-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 460 240" width="460" height="240" role="img" aria-label="Double descent: classical U-curve followed by a second descent of test error beyond the interpolation threshold"><g font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="currentColor"><line x1="50" y1="200" x2="440" y2="200" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="50" y1="200" x2="50" y2="20" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="245" y="230" text-anchor="middle">model size / parameters →</text><text x="15" y="110" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90 15 110)">test error →</text><line x1="225" y1="30" x2="225" y2="200" stroke="currentColor" stroke-dasharray="4 4" stroke-width="1"/><text x="225" y="24" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10">interpolation threshold</text><path d="M62 70 C120 150 175 175 215 178 C222 178 225 40 228 178 C270 175 350 110 435 70" fill="none" stroke="#b54300" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="120" y="110" font-size="10">classical regime</text><text x="345" y="110" font-size="10">over-parameterized</text><circle cx="435" cy="70" r="4" fill="#b54300"/><text x="395" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#b54300">2nd descent</text></g></svg>
